title: Deis Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands for managing applications with Deis PaaS.

commands.description: Create a new application on Deis.
commands.scenario: Use when you need to start a new application deployment.

commands.description: List all applications in the current Deis cluster.
commands.scenario: Use to view all deployed applications in your cluster.

commands.description: Get detailed information about a specific application.
commands.scenario: Use to retrieve the status and details of an app.

commands.description: Set configuration environment variables for an app.
commands.scenario: Use to configure environment variables for your application.

commands.description: Remove configuration environment variables from an app.
commands.scenario: Use to delete environment variables you no longer need.
Use to check the running processes of your app.
Use to troubleshoot and monitor application logs.

commands.description: Scale the number of web processes for an application.
commands.scenario: Use to increase or decrease the app's web process count.

commands.description: Destroy an application and its resources.
commands.scenario: Use to remove an application you no longer need.

commands.description: Deploy an image from a Docker registry to Deis.
commands.scenario: Use to deploy a Docker image directly to Deis.

commands.description: List users with access to an application.
commands.scenario: Use to audit user access to your application.

commands.description: Grant a user access to an application.
commands.scenario: Use to add a collaborator to your application.
